reason: Response A's blend of plain warmth, concrete grounding advice ("Drink some water, eat something that isn't sad, put on the show you've seen a hundred times"), and conversational rhythm matches my actual register when comforting someone. Response B is more conspicuously literary and aphoristic ("Suffering is just love with nowhere to land yet," "Wednesday exists because Tuesday could not hold on") — that polished, essayistic density reads like an imitator over-indexing on lyricism rather than the grounded, practical empathy I tend to default to.
